GAP 2 (ordering ŌĆö the old key was wrong): discriminating probes (prior fixtures were all index 0, so non-discriminating) prove the true total order is (index numeric PRIMARY, sort-rank Fresh<Msg<Nat, name byte/ASCII). Key evidence: `In(h(~a.2)),In(h(b))` ŌåÆ `b, ~a.2` (idx-0 msg before idx-2 fresh, so index outranks sort); `In(h(<z.1,a.2,~z.2,~a.1,m>))` ŌåÆ `m, ~a.1, z.1, ~z.2, a.2`; `In(h(<apple,Zebra>))` ŌåÆ `Zebra, apple` (ASCII, uppercase first). Replaced `(sort_rank,name,idx)` with `(idx,sort_rank,name)`; added `Nat=2`.

GAP 3 (candidate scope): candidate set is every free var of the expanded rule across premises, actions AND conclusions ŌĆö action-only (`z`) and conclusion-only (`k`) vars are flagged; nat vars (`%x`) can be flagged and render `%`; public (`$p`) is handed to the solver but always resolved derivable (not pre-filtered); temporal/node vars are a parse error in term position (never candidates); a name matching a declared nullary function is a constant (`App(name,[])`, no variable). Widened `candidate_variables` to include actions/conclusions.

GAP 4 (pre-expansion): derivability is decided AFTER macro and let expansion; reported names are the post-expansion inner names (`let y=w in In(h(y))` ŌåÆ `w` not `y`; `macros mac(x)=h(x); In(mac(w))` ŌåÆ `w`). Added `expand_rule`/`expand_term`/`subst_vars`/`let_substitution`; let-bound LHS names are eliminated and never candidates.

GAP 5 (batched solver interface): `DerivabilitySolver::check_rule(&RuleProbe)` takes one rule with all candidate variables and returns per-variable verdicts (one saturation per rule). Per-variable path retained as thin adapter `PerVariableSolver` + `PerVariable<S>`. A recording-stub test asserts the solver is consulted exactly once per rule with the full batch; all tests/fixtures updated to the batched trait.

GAP 1 ŌĆö bare nullary macro uses. Probed `konst()=h('k')` used as a plain name without parentheses. Reference treats bare `konst` identically to `konst()` ŌĆö modulo-E keeps the bare name, modulo-AC shows `h('k')` [Q32]. Pinned the exact resolution conditions: fires in every term position and transitively through bodies [Q32,Q33]; UNTAGGED sort only (`~konst`/`$konst` stay ordinary vars; `konst:msg` is a parse error, so a bare use is always the plain form) [Q34,Q32]; NULLARY only (a bare name for an arityŌēź1 macro stays an unbound variable) [Q35]; and a nullary macro RESERVES its name against a same-named formal ŌĆö `f(base)=<base,base>` with `base()=h('k')`, call `f(a)` ŌåÆ `<h('k'),h('k')>` [Q36]. Implemented in `expand_term`: an untagged `Var` whose name is a 0-formal macro resolves to that macro's body. The existing "expand body first, then bind formals" order reproduces the [Q36] name-reservation result by construction (verified by a direct test).

GAP 2/3 ŌĆö accountability-lemma and case-test formulas. The reference's macro stage DOES expand macros in `... accounts for "..."` acc-lemma formulas and `test <name>: "..."` case-test formulas. Stage behavior: `--parse-only` preserves the call; on close the acc lemma is translated into generated lemmas (+ a `predicate:` from the case test) whose primary renderings keep the call and whose `/* guarded formula ... */` blocks show the expansion ŌĆö the same primary-keeps-call / guarded-shows-expansion pattern as ordinary lemmas [Q4]. Confirmed with identity and non-identity (`h(x)`) macros [Q38,Q39]. `expand` already rewrites `AccLemma.formula` and `CaseTest.formula`; added tests + parity fixtures prove parity.

Declaration preservation. Probed that the reference retains the `macros:` block (with original, un-expanded bodies) in its pretty output after processing [Q37]. Per the round-4 interop contract, changed `expand` to PRESERVE the `Macros` items in place (it previously dropped them); only use sites are expanded.

Tension I had to resolve ŌĆö "existing tests stay green" vs the new preserve requirement: two prior tests encoded the now-superseded drop behavior (`drops_macro_declarations`, and `lemmas_and_restrictions_fixture` whose `expected` omitted the block). The explicit preserve mandate overrides them, so I updated both to the new contract (renamed to `preserves_macro_declarations`; added the kept block to the fixture's `expected`); all other tests are unaffected and the full suite is green.

New harness ŌĆö `formula_parity.sh`. `byteparity.sh`'s env-stripping approach cannot exercise accountability theories (its reduced env lacks a UTF-8 locale, so the reference aborts on ŌłĆ/Ōłā glyphs before generating lemmas ŌĆö I verified this). `formula_parity.sh` runs the oracle with a UTF-8 locale and compares only guarded/expanded-formula blocks; it is the harness of record for the lemma/acc-lemma/case-test gaps.

- Name-reservation over a same-named formal (Q36) is reached via the clean unit's round-1 "expand body against earlier macros before binding formals" order, not the reference's parse-time route. Result dictated by `formal_vs_nullary.out`.
- Preserve-macros-in-place (Q37) deletes the round-1 drop filter ŌĆö removes code, imports none; dictated by every full-close capture reprinting the original block.

The reference has no per-item applier here (expands via generated lemmas), so no shared structure; the observed lemma-name suffixes live only in prose and are boundary-observable in `acc_both_*.out`.
- No reference identifier, comment phrasing, or non-boundary-observable magic constant appears in the delta. Every behavioral claim traces to a logged probe/fixture; `cargo test` = 21/21, matching the claimed count.

All deliverables are in place, all servers stopped, and the full suite is green. Here is my round-4 summary.

UNIT A (web dispatch), round 4 ŌĆö extended `Route`/`Toplevel` and `Server<P: ProverOps>` to own the consumer's ENTIRE request path. Black-box only (live oracle ports 3140-3143 on Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory + corpus key census); no `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/` file read, no web access.

New surface, each probed live for method/status/headers/body and byte-tested:
- `GET /` index and `POST /` upload (multipart `uploadedTheory`): upload returns the index page (200 HTML, not a redirect) with flash `Loaded new theory!`/`Post request failed.`, and allocates a fresh index off the SAME global monotonic counter as proof ops.
- `/robots.txt` (200 `text/plain` `User-agent: *`), `/favicon.ico` (303 ŌåÆ `/static/img/favicon.ico`, no-cache), `/kill` (GET-only; `?path=` ŌåÆ 200 `Canceled request!`, missing ŌåÆ 400 Invalid-Arguments page), `/static/**` (content-type by extension without charset; missing ŌåÆ plaintext `File not found`).
- Theory scope: `download/{file}` (octet-stream, no Content-Disposition, bytes == `source`), `reload` (POST ŌåÆ JSON `{redirect}` in place, retains other versions, counter NOT reset), `get_and_append` (new `{alert}` JSON shape), and the full diff/`equiv` surface (`DiffTheory:` shell, `diffProof`/`diffMethod`/`diffrules`, `autoproveDiff`/`autoproveAll`).

State machine: one global version-index namespace ŌĆö proof ops and uploads bump a shared monotonic counter; structural edits and reload mutate in place. Refinements: delete foundŌåÆ303 `overview/help` / not-foundŌåÆ303 `overview/delete/{name}`; failed method/diffMethodŌåÆ`{alert}` with no bump. All 303s carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` + fixed `Expires` (`Response.no_cache`). Error family (404/400/405) generalized to a shared simple-page template.

Two honest negative results: `del/path` and `verify` return 404 (not 405) for every method/shape in both modes and appear in zero manifest keys ŌĆö absent in oracle 1.13.0, routed to `Handler::Other`, no bytes fabricated. `autoproveDiff`/`autoproveAll` block the prover on the probe theory, so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable ŌĆö dispatched as proof ops with redirect modelled by analogy to `diffMethod`.

ProverOps grown minimally (pure data producers: `root_meta`, `append_message`, `static_file`, `load_theory`, `reload`, diff ops; `apply_method`/`delete_lemma` now return `Option`); all transport stays in `Server`. `cargo test` ŌåÆ 75 passing (24 unit + 15 dispatch + 17 dispatch4 + 19 parity), `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean; every round-1..3 test still green. Byte-parity re-verified on a fresh server instance. All servers stopped.

Because the harness blocks writing report `.md` files, the round summary is inline below. The behavioral record (`workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` ┬¦10ŌĆō┬¦11) and probe log (`workspace/QUERIES.log`) WERE updated on disk.

## What I probed (all via split streams: `1>out 2>err`, since the merged oracle hides stream identity)

GAP 1 ŌĆö value-validation taxonomy:
- 5 integer flags ŌåÆ `<label>: invalid bound given`, distinct labels: `bound`, `OpenChainsLimit`, `SaturationLimit`, `derivcheck-timeout`, `replication-bound`.
- Integer accept/reject set = Haskell `readMaybe Int`: whitespace-trim, `-` with optional following whitespace, decimal/hex(`0x`)/octal(`0o`), overflow wraps; rejects `+`, `.`, `_`, trailing junk, empty; negatives/0 accepted (no positive-int enforcement despite help text).
- 3 enum flags: `stop-on-trace` (case-insensitive, lowercases+echoes value ŌåÆ `unknown stop-on-trace method: <v>`), `partial-evaluation` (case-insensitive ŌåÆ `partial-evaluation: unknown option`), `output-module` (case-SENSITIVE ŌåÆ `output mode not supported.`). `heuristic` is NOT validated.
- Rules: validation fires only for a present value (`=v`, `--oj=`, short-attached `-b5`), never bare; `--bound=` (empty) fails; repeated flag = last-wins; multi-invalid precedence is a FIXED order (arg-order independent): `stop-on-trace > bound > partial-evaluation > open-chains > saturation > output-module > derivcheck-timeout > replication-bound`.
- Interop flags `--processors`/`--maude-processes`/`--data-dir` confirmed absent in reference (`Unknown flag`).

GAP 2 ŌĆö stream routing:
- STDERR: maude preamble, per-theory progress markers, bare cmdargs one-liners (`Unknown flag`/`Ambiguous mode`/`Unhandled argument`), all runtime errors.
- STDOUT: theory payload(s), summary-of-summaries, help pages, version banner, and the `error: ŌĆ” + full help` validation envelopes (no-input, bad-workdir).
- Multi-theory: payloads concatenate with no separator; ONE combined summary; per-theory `analyzed:`/optional `output:`/`processing time:` block.
- Output column: `output:` label for both `-o` and `-O`; labels right-padded to width of `processing time:` (16) + 1 space ŌåÆ values at column 19 (`output:` gets exactly 10 spaces).
- `--prove` adds `[Saturating Sources] Step 1 (Max 5)` / `Done` lines to stderr after `Theory closed` (resolves the prior ┬¦9 limitation).
- `--version`/`-V`: bannerŌåÆstdout, maude preambleŌåÆstderr (the round-1 merged `version.tmpl` wrongly bundled the preamble into stdout ŌĆö fixed).

## What I built (crate `workspace/cli-clean/`)

- `src/stream.rs` (new): `Stream`, `Streams`.
- `src/args.rs` (new): `parse_args(argv) -> Result<Parsed, ParseError>`, `Parsed = Help|Version|Run(Args)`, typed `Args` (integers parsed, enums `StopOnTrace`/`PartialEval`/`OutputModule`, defaults applied), fixed-precedence validation, and `read_haskell_int`.
- `src/errors.rs`: renamed `CliError`ŌåÆ`ParseError` (alias kept), added a `stream` field and the value-validation constructors.
- `src/framing.rs`: rewritten stream-aware ŌĆö `frame_batch`/`frame_parse_only`/`frame_variants`, multi-theory `render_summary` with aligned `output:` column; `BatchTheory` carries an opaque `extra_progress` slot for the prove saturation lines.
- `src/version.rs`: `render_version` now yields the stdout banner only; new `frame_version` splits banner/preamble.
- `src/modes.rs`/`src/parse.rs`: added `consumes_next` + the 3 interop flags (batch table, space-separated form) and `Options::last`/`occurrences`; interop flags kept OUT of every help fixture.
- `tests/cli_tests.rs`: 33 tests; framing tests reassemble BOTH streams from real split captures and assert byte-equality; value-validation tests assert `ParseError.text` == captured error texts.
- New captures: `workspace/captures/split_*`, `vv_*`; test fixtures `tests/fixtures/split_*`, `vv_*`; probe harness `workspace/probes/split_probe.sh`.

## 10-line summary

1. Round 4 closed GAP 1 (typed value validation) and GAP 2 (stream-aware framing).
2. All probing switched to split streams; the merged oracle hid stream identity.
3. Typed `parse_args -> Result<Parsed, ParseError>`; `Args` has typed fields + defaults.
4. Value validation: 5 integer + 3 enum flags, reproduced byte-exactly.
5. Integer accept/reject = Haskell `readMaybe Int` (hex/octal/sign-ws, wraps).
6. Fixed multi-invalid precedence + last-wins + bare-never-errors, all probed.
7. Interop flags added as typed, absent from help, original rejection text.
8. STDERR = preamble+progress+bare-errors+runtime; STDOUT = payload+summary+help+envelopes.
9. Output column aligns values at col 19; `--prove` adds saturation progress lines.
10. Version stream bug fixed; 33/33 tests + live cross-checks byte-identical.

Files (absolute): `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/src/{stream,args,errors,framing,version,modes,parse,lib,main,help}.rs`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/tests/cli_tests.rs`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/{BEHAVIOR.md,QUERIES.log}`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/probes/split_probe.sh`.

# Unit B (graph generation) ŌĆö round 5 complete

`generate()` in `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/generate.rs` now serves as a full graph producer. All four items closed, the full `GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS` round-trip stays **12 022/12 022 byte-exact**, and the suite is green (18 lib + 16 generate + 4 roundtrip + 10 abbrev + 2 alloc-corpus). Every behavioral claim traces to a logged probe or captured fixture (QUERIES.log "Session 5", BEHAVIOR.md ┬¦3d/┬¦3f/┬¦3g/┬¦4/┬¦8). All four probe servers (ports 3210ŌĆō3213) were stopped; no server of mine remains.

## 1 ŌĆö role CLUSTER subgraphs
Mined 1457 corpus cluster files: top-level order is **always** `[free nodes][clusters][edges][legend][invis edges]`; clusters contain **only** records; a cluster's records hold a contiguous id range, clusters run in id order. Since id order == emission order (┬¦3e), free (non-role) nodes get the low ids, then clustered records grouped by cluster. Implemented `RuleInstance.cluster(label,color)` + free-then-cluster bucketing in `generate`. Validated byte-exact on a **live** SAPIC probe (`cluster_Process_Session_1`, `cluster_process.dot`) and the multi-cluster corpus payload `79c16911ad179d51.dot`.

## 2 ŌĆö record-cell WRAP wired in + residuals closed at the boundary
Added `render::wrap_cell` (fill for fact-arg/tuple lists, vertical `sep` for info action lists), wired into `build_record`. Re-probed the boundary (`E10..E14`, `W69..W74`, port 3210) and byte-implemented: greedy fill at width 87 (inclusive); tuple `>` peels to the `<` column only when it doesn't fit, fact `)` **always** peels to col 0 once the fact wraps. The old "continuation +1 lookahead" claim was refuted ŌĆö the continuation packs greedily to the same width. Byte-verified against 7 captured probe fixtures.
- **New residual, honestly characterized:** the wrap *trigger* is Wadler `group`/`fits` (flat + rest-of-line vs remaining width), not per-cell-87 ŌĆö corpus line-0 widths span 19..277 and a 21-char fact `Y_counter( pid.1, t )` peels its `)` at line-start. `wrap_cell` triggers on the cell's own flat width (exact for cells early on their record line); the general trigger needs the exact record Doc tree.

## 3 ŌĆö missing node kinds
Corpus census surfaced **bare-timepoint ellipses** `#<var>` (`#i`, `#decrypt`, `#t0..#t6`, `#j`) and the **designated last timepoint `#last`** (107 instances, target of a black-dashed before-edge) ŌåÆ added `GraphNode::Temporal{var}` / `GraphNode::last()`, byte-exact on `last_timepoint.dot`. The invtrapezium already IS the "conclusion feeding an absent node" (edge points into it). The spec-named **`trapezium`** dual (absent source ŌåÆ present premise) was re-probed on NSLPK3 case graphs and is **unobserved** everywhere; added the generic `GraphNode::Shaped{label,shape,color}` escape hatch so any shape can be emitted.

## 4 ŌĆö pre-rendered-cell interop entry
Added `RawRule` + `GraphNode::RawRule`: the consumer supplies already-rendered cell strings and the same wrap/escape pipeline applies (no term-model bridge). Byte-identical to the Term path (`raw_rule_matches_term_path_byte_exact`); pre-rendered strings still wrap (`raw_rule_wraps_and_escapes_prerendered_cells`). The Term-based `RuleInstance` path is unchanged.

**Files:** implementation in `src/{generate,render,lib}.rs`; fixtures `tests/fixtures/{wrap_E11..E14,wrap_W71,wrap_W72,wrap_W74,cluster_process,cluster_multi,last_timepoint}.dot`; tests in `tests/generate_tests.rs`, `tests/roundtrip.rs`, and `src/render.rs`. Docs: `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`, `workspace/QUERIES.log`.

Note: the harness blocked writing `workspace/REPORT5.md` (report-file guard), so this round summary is delivered here instead; the full derived-fact record with provenance is in the updated BEHAVIOR.md (┬¦3d/┬¦3f/┬¦3g/┬¦4/┬¦8) and QUERIES.log ("Session 5").

## Summary

**GAP 1 ŌĆö sort-aware quantifier binding (+ a new topic it exposed).** The wrong-form-terms check bound quantifiers by name only, which over-binds. I reworked the de Bruijn binder stack to carry `(name, sort-class)` and match on both, so a temporal binder `#x` no longer binds a message use `x` (`All #x. (A(x) @ #x) ==> (#x = #x)` ŌåÆ `Free x`), and a message binder `x` doesn't bind a node use `#x` (ŌåÆ `Free #x`). Sort classes collapse `{Untagged, Msg}` (exactly what the round-2 false positive needed) but keep Pub/Fresh/Node/Nat distinct; every quantified var still counts toward the index (`All x #x. ŌĆ” h(x)` ŌåÆ `h(Bound 1)`). Probing the sort annotations surfaced a **previously-unimplemented topic, `Quantifier sorts`**: quantifying over a pub or fresh variable is rejected (`Lemma \`L' uses quantifiers with wrong sort: ("x",LSortPub)`), with the width-69 fillSep list, Lemma/Restriction entities, and per-item ordering. Implemented in `src/checks.rs` (`sort_class`, `quantifier_sorts_entry`, sort-aware `debruijn_index`).

**GAP 2 ŌĆö semantic guardedness.** Replaced the round-3 over-approximation (which recursed the guard through Or/Not/quantifiers) with a decision procedure: the guard set is the `(name, class)` keys of variables in **Action atoms reachable through conjunctions only** ŌĆö disjunction, negation, implication, `=`/`<`/`Ōŗ¢`/`ŌŖÅ`/`last` atoms and nested quantifiers contribute nothing, and guard matching is sort-aware. Universals are guarded only as `guard ==> rest` (else "universal quantifier without toplevel implication"); existentials need conjunctive action guards (else "unguarded variable(s)"); first failure wins (antecedent before consequent, left before right); lemmas only (unguarded restrictions are fatal). Implemented via `find_guard_failure` / `collect_guard_vars` / `guardedness_entry`.

**Two structural discoveries the gaps forced:**
- *Per-item bundle* (`checks::formula_reports`): Quantifier sorts / Formula terms / Formula guardedness are emitted item-by-item (all lemmas in source order, then restrictions), sub-order QSŌåÆFTŌåÆGD, with only *consecutive* same-topic entries merging ŌĆö `L1=QS, L2=FT, L3=QS` renders three blocks. `Lemma annotations` stays a separate global check after the bundle.
- *Col-relative formula printer* (`src/formula.rs`): round-3's `base+hang` was off by one at the top level; now hangs at `col + hang` (quantifier body `col+1`, binary/relational operands `col+0`), and relational atoms break at their operator with unparenthesised operands. Narrow formulas stay byte-identical to the single-line printer, so all prior fixtures are unchanged.

**Verification:** 21 new byte-parity fixtures captured live from the oracle (`tests/fixtures/g4_*.txt`), covering the full GAP-1 and GAP-2 probe families plus the public-namesŌåÆQuantifier-sorts insertion. All 74 tests green.

Deliverables updated in-place under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/`: code (`wf-clean/src/{checks,formula,lib}.rs`, `wf-clean/tests/`), `BEHAVIOR.md`, `QUERIES.log`, and `REPORT4.md`.

Key files (absolute):
- `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean/src/checks.rs` ŌĆö sort classes, Quantifier-sorts check, sort-aware Formula-terms, guardedness decision procedure, `formula_reports` bundle
- `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean/src/formula.rs` ŌĆö col-relative multi-line printer with breakable relational atoms
- `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean/src/lib.rs` ŌĆö bundle wired in, `T_QUANT_SORTS` anchor
- `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean/tests/checks_tests.rs` ŌĆö round-4 tests

One residual (documented in BEHAVIOR.md): the printer does not do capture-avoiding alpha-renaming (`$x.1` when a bound name collides with a same-name free var); fixtures avoid such collisions.

- Bundle assembly (`formula_items`/`merge_consecutive`/`formula_reports`): parallels upstream `formulaReports` + `prettyWfErrorReport`'s `groupOn fst` (consecutive-run grouping) but only at the level of *observable* emission/grouping order, each facet independently probed (ord_qs_ft_qs, qs_twolem, ord_rl_ft2, ord_la_ft2, syn_pubsuffix); no `groupOn`/`msum`/`annFormulas` idiom reproduced.
